unittest/TestLoader/errors.name: errors
unittest/TestLoader/errors.description: A list of the non-fatal errors encountered while loading tests. Not reset by the loader at any point. Fatal errors are signalled by the relevant method raising an exception to the caller. Non-fatal errors are also indicated by a synthetic test that will raise the original error when run. Added in version 3.5.

info.excerpt: A list of the non-fatal errors encountered while loading tests. Not reset by the loader at any point. Fatal errors are signalled by the relevant method raising an exception to the caller. Non-fatal er...

unittest/TestResult/errors.name: errors
unittest/TestResult/errors.description: A list containing 2-tuples of TestCase instances and strings holding formatted tracebacks. Each tuple represents a test which raised an unexpected exception.

info.excerpt: A list containing 2-tuples of TestCase instances and strings holding formatted tracebacks. Each tuple represents a test which raised an unexpected exception.

unittest/TestResult/expectedFailures.name: expectedFailures
unittest/TestResult/expectedFailures.description: A list containing 2-tuples of TestCase instances and strings holding formatted tracebacks. Each tuple represents an expected failure or error of the test case.

info.excerpt: A list containing 2-tuples of TestCase instances and strings holding formatted tracebacks. Each tuple represents an expected failure or error of the test case.

unittest/enterModuleContext.name: enterModuleContext
unittest/enterModuleContext.description: Enter the supplied context manager. If successful, also add its __exit__() method as a cleanup function by addModuleCleanup() and return the result of the __enter__() method. Added in version 3.11.

info.excerpt: Enter the supplied context manager. If successful, also add its __exit__() method as a cleanup function by addModuleCleanup() and return the result of the __enter__() method. Added in version 3.11.

unittest/expectedFailure.name: expectedFailure
unittest/expectedFailure.description: Mark the test as an expected failure or error. If the test fails or errors in the test function itself (rather than in one of the test fixture methods) then it will be considered a success. If the test passes, it will be considered a failure.

unittest/IsolatedAsyncioTestCase/enterAs.name: enterAsyncContext
unittest/IsolatedAsyncioTestCase/enterAs.description: Enter the supplied asynchronous context manager. If successful, also add its __aexit__() method as a cleanup function by addAsyncCleanup() and return the result of the __aenter__() method. Added in version 3.11.

unittest/TestCase/enterClassContext.name: enterClassContext
unittest/TestCase/enterClassContext.description: Enter the supplied context manager. If successful, also add its __exit__() method as a cleanup function by addClassCleanup() and return the result of the __enter__() method. Added in version 3.11.

info.excerpt: Enter the supplied context manager. If successful, also add its __exit__() method as a cleanup function by addClassCleanup() and return the result of the __enter__() method. Added in version 3.11.

unittest/TestCase/enterContext.name: enterContext
unittest/TestCase/enterContext.description: Enter the supplied context manager. If successful, also add its __exit__() method as a cleanup function by addCleanup() and return the result of the __enter__() method. Added in version 3.11.

info.excerpt: Enter the supplied context manager. If successful, also add its __exit__() method as a cleanup function by addCleanup() and return the result of the __enter__() method. Added in version 3.11.
